Title: FAX1, a Novel Membrane Protein Mediating Plastid Fatty Acid Export

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tmemb_14 superfamily protein; UPF0136; chloroplast inner envelope membrane protein with α-helical transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates plastid fatty acid export; loss reduces ER-derived lipids, TAG oils, C29 ketone wax, biomass, and male fertility, while overexpression increases TAG oils and biomass
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Triacylglycerol oils; cuticular ketone waxes; fatty acid-derived pollen wall materials
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; flowers; stem epidermal cuticle; anther/pollen outer wall
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-rich storage oils; protective cuticular wax barrier; pollen wall/coat formation required for male fertility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; complementation; 35S overexpression; GFP localization; immunoblotting; lipidomics/mass spectrometry; wax analysis; microscopy/TEM; yeast transport complementation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present throughout development; peaks in leaf tissues and early pollen development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in cauline leaves, sepals, and early pollen development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth conditions; yeast assays with α-linolenic acid or cerulenin plus exogenous fatty acids
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LACS4 (co-expression only, not direct physical interaction)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: DNA microarray, ArrayExpress E-MTAB-3090; lipidomics by UPLC-Orbitrap MS and UPLC-qTOF MS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe male sterility and reduced biomass; altered lipid homeostasis across tissues
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to increase plant oil/biofuel production; could be stacked with FA synthesis, acyl-editing, or transport genes with seed-specific deployment to minimize developmental penalties
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAX1 encodes a chloroplast inner envelope membrane protein of the Tmemb_14 family. 2. Genetic loss and gain of function demonstrate that FAX1 controls plastid fatty acid export and cellular lipid homeostasis. 3. FAX1 is required for normal pollen exine/pollen coat formation, stem ketone wax accumulation, biomass production, and strongly influences TAG oil accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although background genes in acyl lipid metabolism were discussed and transcriptionally altered, the Results section functionally centered on FAX1 as the experimentally validated core gene; pea Ps-FAX1 was cloned for comparative/localization work but metabolic phenotypes were tested in Arabidopsis.
